mapping.b_element: imposed inhomogeneous 'temperature' T across the lattice

mapping.a_element: formation of superconducting islands (spatial ordering) above the mean-field transition
mapping.b_element: formation of segregated domains (spatial ordering) on the lattice

brief_justification: The overlap is only vocabulary and setting (a spatial lattice with an inhomogeneous 'temperature' and domain ordering); the driving machinery differs decisively. Paper A's mechanism is quenched disorder producing a static, disorder-averaged probability distribution of local transition temperatures and a percolative network of superconducting islands, whereas Paper B's mechanism is a dynamical feedback between segregation and temperature that self-organizes the average T. No shared governing process (no shared disorder-induced Tc distribution, no shared feedback/self-organization) yields a coherent role-for-role mapping.
